metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4008
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1J, 1K. shRNA-mediated knock-down of SCRN1 in dissociated hippocampal neuron cultures results in impaired binding of a synaptotagmin antibody to the luminal region of synaptotagmin, which is only exposed to the extracellular media during synaptic vesicle cycling. Synaptic vesicle cycling was stimulated in neuronal cultures by applying 50 uM bicuculline for 10 min at 37°C. Antibody used was mouse anti-synaptotagmin (SySy, 105311, clone 604.2).

18/01/2021 Dnyanada: Using expansion microscopy, SCRN1knock-down demonstrates an altered ER nanoscale organization in terms of discontinuity of the ER tubules in axons, and overall less dense and irregular ER structures in axons, dendrites, and soma. FRAP experiments expressing SCRN1 knock-down also demonstrate discontinuous ER structures (~40% reduction in ER marker TagRFP-ER) indicating impaired ER dynamics. Based on this and the results from the synaptotagmin uptake assay (as described in the annotation), the term "regulation of synaptic vesicle cycle" was used.. Experimental description: Dissociated hippocampal and cortical neuron cultures were prepared from embryonic day 18 rat pups of mixed gender.
